name: Remote MCP OAuth Client Credentials
description: Single-stage workflow that proves a specialist can use an OAuth client-credentials remote MCP server.
outcome: A single specialist retrieves the deterministic reference answer through an OAuth-backed remote MCP server and publishes proof.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `oauth-search`. Treat that stage as the only workflow checkpoint: `oauth-search` exists to prove that a specialist can use a granted OAuth-backed remote MCP server and still close the workflow cleanly. Use this role deliberately: `oauth-search-specialist` owns the entire work item. The orchestrator only needs to route that specialist, then close the work when the specialist has produced the artifact and a structured handoff.
Happy path: dispatch `oauth-search-specialist`, require the specialist to use the granted OAuth-backed remote MCP tools, write `deliverables/oauth-search-proof.md`, upload it as an artifact, and submit one structured handoff that repeats the final answer. The answer line MUST read `Answer: Victoria` and the proof MUST include one `Source:` line.
If the specialist cannot reach the OAuth-backed remote MCP server, cannot complete token-backed discovery, or tries to bypass the remote MCP capability, require a corrective retry or escalate with a precise reason. Do not silently substitute another tool path, do not invent reviews or approvals, and do not close the workflow without proof that a remote MCP tool was actually used. Do not keep the workflow open for non-blocking formatting noise once the remote MCP result and artifact exist; record that as a callout instead.
Completion rules: finish only after `oauth-search-specialist` has produced the artifact, repeated the answer in the final handoff, and left a usable source line. Guided closure rule: treat the authored flow as best-intent process guidance. Preferred use of the granted remote MCP server must be followed, but the orchestrator may waive a weaker preferred detail with a recorded reason when the legal OAuth-backed answer already exists. Smaller deviations must be recorded as callouts instead of preventing closure.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: oauth-search
stages.goal: A deterministic OAuth-backed remote MCP answer is delivered with proof.
